title: Centre cracks whip on Meta over Instagram ads promoting child sexual abuse material
content: India's IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw has ordered officials to summon Meta following reports of Instagram advertisements featuring child sexual abuse material. This action highlights growing concerns about objectionable content on social media and the effectiveness of content moderation on platforms like Instagram. The ministry is seeking answers regarding the platform's handling of such serious issues.

title: Twist in Ketan Agarwal case: Police detain Chetan's classmate who 'knew' of murder plot
content: A new development in the Ketan Agarwal murder probe sees police questioning a youth from Beed, identified as Chetan Chaudhary's friend. Investigators believe Chaudhary and Siya Goyal confided their alleged murder plan to him. The youth, who reportedly advised against the act, was contacted by both accused after the incident. Agarwal died after a fall from Lohagad Fort on June 18.

title: 'Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is as good as Tendulkar and Kohli': Kapil Dev amid debut calls
content: Excitement is building for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's potential India debut against England. Despite a sensational IPL, he awaits his international bow. Legendary Kapil Dev, while acknowledging his immense talent akin to Tendulkar and Kohli, urges caution against excessive hype, emphasising the need for him to prove himself in longer formats.  The debate intensifies as India seeks a turnaround in the T20 series.

title: Amazon to hire 11,000 interns: 3 qualities that AWS CEO says the company is looking for
content: Amazon is prioritizing adaptability, curiosity, and rapid learning for its 11,000 interns this year, a shift driven by AI's growing role. AWS CEO Matt Garman highlights that fresh talent brings essential energy and new perspectives, crucial for innovation and cost reduction. This focus ensures the company cultivates future leaders capable of navigating an evolving tech landscape.

title: Crude cools down: What falling oil prices mean for your wallet and markets — explained in 10 charts
content: Global crude prices have cooled, bringing relief to India's economy. While consumers may not see immediate fuel price drops due to inventory costs, the nation benefits from a reduced import bill, easing inflation, and improved government finances. Sectors like aviation are already seeing positive impacts, and economic growth forecasts are being upgraded, signaling a brighter outlook.

title: 'They're best by a mile': Pak coach calls out India obsession in judging his team
content: Pakistan coach Mike Hesson argues his team's progress shouldn't be solely judged by results against India, highlighting the current Indian side's dominance over every team. He pointed out Pakistan's strong performance in other matches, emphasizing their significant improvement in win percentages over the past year, moving from a low standing to winning a majority of their games.

title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, while scrapping thousands of open roles.  The company is heavily investing in AI infrastructure, aiming for efficiency amidst a competitive landscape.  This stance contrasts with other industry leaders' more cautious outlooks on AI's impact on employment.

title: 'Pak must stop backing terror': India on why Indus Waters Treaty still 'in abeyance'
content: India's stance on the Indus Waters Treaty remains firm, with the agreement suspended due to Pakistan's continued support for cross-border terrorism. The MEA reiterated this position, emphasizing Pakistan's need to cease its sponsorship of terror. Meanwhile, India has communicated its views on the proposed Teesta River project to Bangladesh, assuring cooperation based on mutual understanding and consultations.

title: Scientists reconstruct 45‑ft mega‑snake; reveals past hotter climate
content: Imagine a serpent as long as a small plane, weighing over a ton! This was Titanoboa, the prehistoric king of the Amazon. Discovered in Colombia, this colossal boa constrictor-like snake, reaching up to 45 feet, once crushed crocodiles and giant fish. Its existence reveals a much warmer ancient Earth, with scientists estimating equatorial temperatures around 91°F. Titanoboa's reign highlights the astonishing diversity of life that once roamed our planet.

title: America's largest bank has a $3.5 billion plan to tackle 'AC hate' of governments across Europe
content: JPMorgan Chase is reportedly close to acquiring French cooling network operator Idex for over $3.5 billion, amid Europe's intensifying heatwaves. This potential deal highlights a growing demand for cooling infrastructure in a region historically resistant to widespread air conditioning. The acquisition would bolster JPMorgan's European presence as rising temperatures fuel the debate over cooling solutions and their political implications.

title: 'Extremely pained & angered': RSS asks SIT to take strict action in Ram Temple row
content: The RSS expressed deep pain and anger over alleged donation embezzlement at the Ayodhya Ram Temple, demanding strict action against culprits. RSS general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale urged the Hindu community to remain vigilant against those exploiting the incident to defame them. He welcomed the SIT probe and stressed the need for transparent temple administration to uphold devotees' faith.

title: Self-immolation outside UN draws fresh attention to Tibet
content: A Tibetan activist's self-immolation outside the UN headquarters in New York has reignited global attention on Tibet's human rights crisis. Lobga Rangzen's desperate act, protesting Chinese rule and new assimilation laws, highlights the fading international focus on the issue. This tragic event, occurring after over 150 similar protests in Tibet, challenges governments to confront the unresolved situation, forcing a difficult conversation amidst strained US-China relations.

title: Big boost to armed forces, defence ministry clears equipment worth Rs 50,000 crore
content: The Defence Ministry has greenlit significant acquisitions exceeding Rs 52,000 crore, bolstering national security. Key approvals include advanced anti-drone systems, indigenous anti-tank missiles, and enhanced air defence capabilities for both Army and Air Force. Naval and Air Force modernization also sees boosts with new mine systems, unmanned aerial vehicles, and high-altitude surveillance platforms, ensuring readiness against evolving threats.

title: Honeymoon murder case: Why SC refused to cancel Sonam Raghuvanshi's bail order
content: The Supreme Court declined to revoke bail for Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused of her husband's murder during their honeymoon. The Meghalaya High Court had granted bail due to a significant clerical error where police cited a non-existent legal section, failing to properly inform her of the murder charge. Despite reservations, the apex court noted her release and adherence to bail conditions.

title: Massive fire at Lucknow restaurant, employees feared trapped; rescue op launched
content: A significant fire engulfed Vijay Paradise restaurant in Lucknow's Khurram Nagar on Friday afternoon, sparking a massive rescue operation. Concerns are high for employees believed to be trapped inside. This incident follows a string of recent fires in the city, including a tragic blaze last week that claimed multiple lives and injured others. Emergency services are working diligently to manage the current situation.

title: Stock market: Which are top 10 gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian equity benchmarks closed higher on Friday, buoyed by positive global sentiment, falling crude oil prices, and anticipation of a softer interest rate environment. The Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex both saw gains, with the Nifty 50 ending the week nearly 1% up. Realty and IT sectors led the gains, while PSU Banks and Auto sectors experienced declines. The rupee also strengthened against the dollar.

title: 'Ireland hangover': Karthik on Shreyas Iyer's toss decision vs England
content: India's surprising decision to bat first in the opening T20I against England, despite overcast conditions, is attributed by Dinesh Karthik to a lingering 'Ireland hangover' from their recent series defeat. The team posted a strong 189/7 before rain abandoned the match. Captain Shreyas Iyer also achieved a personal milestone, crossing 5,000 international runs during his impressive knock.

title: Oil slips to pre-Iran war levels: Is a petrol, diesel price cut coming?
content: Petrol and diesel prices in India have risen by around Rs 7.5 per litre since May 15. The first hike in the retail rates came over two and a half months after the US-Iran war began. Private retailer Nayara Energy has already cut rates of petrol and diesel.

title: Who is Lokhande Prashant Sitaram? IAS officer appointed as new CBSE Chairperson
content: Senior IAS officer Lokhande Prashant Sitaram has been appointed as the new CBSE Chairperson on an additional charge basis while continuing as Additional Secretary in the Ministry of Home Affairs. A 2001-batch AGMUT cadre officer with engineering and industrial management qualifications, he will lead the board until the Centre appoints a regular Chairperson, ensuring continuity in CBSE's functioning.

title: Maharashtra's Uniform Civil Code: Committee to draft legislation in two weeks
content: Maharashtra is set to establish a committee within two weeks to draft legislation for a Uniform Civil Code. This move follows an announcement that the state will implement UCC, with a retired high court judge to head the drafting panel. The UCC aims to create a common legal framework for personal matters like marriage and inheritance, currently in place in Goa, Uttarakhand, and Assam.

title: Germany coach resigns days after shock FIFA WC exit, Klopp tipped as successor
content: The German Football Association (DFB) confirmed Nagelsmann's resignation on Friday, four days after Germany were knocked out by Paraguay in the round of 32 following a 1-1 draw and a penalty shootout.

title: Fake Canada study visa promise costs consultancy Rs 12.35 lakh in consumer case
content: A Chandigarh consumer commission has ordered a visa consultancy and its former employee to refund Rs 12.35 lakh to a 21-year-old woman. The duo provided fake admission documents for a Canadian college, duping the complainant. The commission found them guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practices, mandating a full refund with interest and compensation for harassment.

title: How Ronaldo and Yamal turned Day 22 into Kafka fanfic
content: Day 22 saw the World Cup turn into a Kafka fanfic. Ronaldo became a giant insect, Spain gave Austria a lesson in passing bureaucracy, and Switzerland didn't give Algeria a glimpse of the castle. Read more in today's Offside.

title: Govt orders removal of 3 Chinese apps being 'misused' in remotely disabling e-rickshaws
content: Authorities have removed two smartphone applications, including the Chinese BAT-BMS app, from app stores following reports of e-rickshaws being remotely disabled. These apps, designed for battery management, were allegedly exploited to disrupt vehicles by connecting to unsecured Bluetooth-enabled battery systems. The government is urging app stores to enhance scrutiny to prevent the availability of such potentially harmful applications.

title: EU–US goods trade hits record €875 billion despite tariffs: Study
content: Despite tariffs, EU-US trade hit a record €875 billion last year. However, a new study reveals underlying strain, particularly for Germany's auto sector, which saw a significant export drop. While Ireland's pharmaceutical exports surged, masking regional weakness, services trade also reached new heights, though the EU faced a deficit. The report warns against further tariff threats, emphasizing the need for stability.

title: How Centre aims to reduce dependence on imported gold
content: The proposal has gained traction following multiple meetings held over the past two weeks involving senior government ministers, officials from the Reserve Bank of India, banks and representatives of the gold industry, the report said.

title: A glimpse of future combat aviation: F-15EX, Ghost Bat team up over Pacific
content: The US Air Force is pioneering Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCAs), or loyal wingmen, as part of its sixth-generation fighter program. These autonomous drones, like the MQ-28 Ghost Bat, are being tested in the Pacific alongside manned jets. India is also developing its own loyal wingman, the CATS Warrior, to enhance its fighter fleet's capabilities in future aerial combat scenarios.

title: 102 missed alerts, failed anti-drone system: US report on 2024 Trump shooting
content: A critical report reveals the US Secret Service missed crucial local police radio chatter about the gunman who targeted President Trump in Pennsylvania. The agency also lacked a joint communications hub, receiving minimal alerts. This failure, coupled with an inoperable counter-drone system that missed a pre-attack drone flight, left the President's security vulnerable. The incident resulted in casualties and injuries.

title: 'Dhoni sir is very similar': Deepti compares India's legend to Djokovic
content: Deepti Sharma compared MS Dhoni to Novak Djokovic, saying both legends share an exceptional ability to remain calm under pressure. Attending The Championships, Wimbledon for the first time, Deepti called it a dream come true, named Djokovic her favourite tennis player, and said she would love to watch tennis alongside Sachin Tendulkar, Ravi Shastri and Sunil Gavaskar.

title: Indian Navy sends P-8I to RIMPAC, the world's largest naval exercise
content: India's P-8I aircraft is participating in RIMPAC, the world's largest naval exercise, hosted by the US. This triennial event, themed 'Partners: Integrated and Prepared,' aims to enhance interoperability and readiness among thirty participating nations. The exercise features diverse training scenarios, underscoring the strong, ongoing cooperation between the Indian and US navies in various operational domains and humanitarian efforts.

title: 'We were fortunate': Martinez defends VAR review; Croatia coach laments 'bad refereeing'
content: Portugal coach Roberto Martinez defended the VAR decisions in his side's dramatic 2-1 World Cup win over Croatia, insisting "there were no bad decisions" and backing the use of ball technology. Croatia boss Zlatko Dalic criticised the officiating as "very bad refereeing" but admitted his side had chances to win before Goncalo Ramos' stoppage-time winner.

title: ‎He failed 35 govt exams before cracking UPSC: Read the inspiring story of Vijay Vardhan
content: Vijay Vardhan's extraordinary journey saw him fail 35 government exams before achieving his dream. After years of relentless effort and heartbreak, he finally cracked the UPSC, first becoming an IPS officer in 2018 and then an IAS officer in 2021 with an impressive rank. His story is a testament to unparalleled perseverance, inspiring countless aspirants facing similar challenges in India's competitive exam landscape.

title: 'Police regime': Stalin slams Tamil Nadu CM Vijay over ex DMK MLA's arrest
content: Former Chief Minister MK Stalin has slammed his successor, accusing the government of running an "action cinema-style police regime." Stalin claims the administration prioritizes arresting opposition MLAs over addressing rising crime rates, including sexual assault cases. He alleges the Chief Minister is using arrests to silence critics and maintain power through questionable means, rather than focusing on public welfare.

title: Why Delhi's EV policy poses big risk if adopted by other states
content: Delhi's new EV Policy 2026 aims to accelerate clean mobility by phasing out petrol and CNG two-wheelers by April 2028 and mandating electric three-wheelers from 2027. While offering substantial incentives, a Morgan Stanley report warns that wider adoption by other states could pose a significant challenge for automakers, especially in the two-wheeler segment. The policy includes Rs 70 billion in direct incentives and aims to install over 30,000 charging points.

title: Ammonia removal operation at Tiruvallur seafood processing plant on July 4 and 5
content: A 500-metre restricted zone has been set up for the operation, which is being conducted in light of the ammonia leak at the plant

title: Legislative debates must focus on solutions, not criticism: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla
content: Lok Sabha Secretariat, and the Parliamentary Research and Training Institute for Democracies (PRIDE), in collaboration with the West Bengal Legislative Assembly is organising the two-day orientation programme for newly-elected members

title: Government to summon Meta over Instagram ads promoting child sexual abuse material
content: The Centre had also asked Meta to explain why action shouldn't be initiated under the IT Act and rules over WhatsApp's new feature that may increase cybercrimes

title: India-Japan ties should not target third party: China
content: India and Japan also expressed "serious concern" at the situation in the East China Sea and South China Sea, and opposed unilateral actions that endanger freedom of navigation as well as attempts to change the status quo by force

title: ORR facelift design to be discussed with residents and companies before works commence
content: The Minister inspected the spot, and his visit was part of discussions on the comprehensive redevelopment of ORR from Silk Board Junction to K.R. Puram Metro Station at an estimated cost of ₹450 crore

title: Lokesh lays foundation for ₹6.30-crore works at Mangalagiri Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy temple
content: The works include a new Yagasala and the renovation of the Vahanasala, Ghanta Mandapam and Sri Krishna Devaraya Mandapam; the Minister asked officials to draw up a master plan for the Mangalagiri temple

title: T.N. Governor conducting review meeting with officials unacceptable: CPI(M)
content: “If the Governor has views on any issue, he should take it up with the Chief Minister and sort it out. But acting arbitrarily goes against the power given to the Governor by the Constitution,” CPI(M) State secretary P. Shanmugam said

title: Bandra is disappearing: Is this what Mumbai calls development?
content: The suburb is changing fast. Will redevelopment transform Mumbai into a city of identical towers instead of distinct neighbourhoods?

title: Industry-integrated training vital for AI-driven job market: APSCHE chief
content: Madhu Murthy says statewide initiative will equip 50,000 students with AI skills; 20 receive offer letters under Cohort 2025-26

title: DMK Gen Z wing leader Anbanantham approaches Madras High Court seeking anticipatory bail
content: The Krishnagiri police had booked him under the Indecent Representation of Women (Prohibition) Act pursuant to his Instagram videos against Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay, the latter’s family members and an actress

title: Progress slow on Comprehensive Dam Safety Evaluation in Kerala as deadline closes in
content: Owners of dams are required to complete the first CDSE by an independent panel of experts within five years of the commencement of the Dam Safety Act, 2021. As per this requirement, the deadline expires on December 31, 2026

title: DMK MLA Anitha Radhakrishnan arrested over remarks against Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
content: The arrest came shortly after the Madras High Court dismissed an anticipatory bail plea filed by Mr. Radhakrishnan in a case registered by the Authoor police

title: Shashi Tharoor recalls past security concerns amid Adani’s proposed MSC stake transfer in Vizhinjam port
content: Congress MP says Vizhinjam project had failed to secure bidders several times under both UPA and NDA governments due to concerns over possible involvement of Chinese companies

title: Watch: When will petrol and diesel prices come down?
content: When will petrol and diesel prices come down? Union Petroleum Minister Hardeep Singh Puri says consumers may have to wait, even though global crude oil prices have eased. He said the fuel currently being sold at retail outlets was refined from crude purchased when the West Asia conflict had pushed prices, freight and insurance costs sharply higher.

title: No truth in claims of SCCL’s 40 lakh tonnes unaccounted coal: Telangana govt
content: Deputy Chief Minister says Telangana government had directed the SCCL Vigilance Director to conduct a full scale inquiry into the issue

title: Indian Army concludes multinational exercise Khaan Quest 2026 in Mongolia
content: Held from June 20 to July 3 at the Five Hills Training Area in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia, the 23rd edition of the exercise brought together more than 1,000 troops from 18 countries to enhance interoperability, strengthen coordination and improve operational readiness for United Nations peace support operations under Chapter VII of the U.N. Charter.

title: CMFRI identifies new deep-sea fish from Arabian Sea
content: Cyttopsis indica was found at depths of 350 m to 500 m along the continental slope of the eastern Lakshadweep Sea

title: Karnataka government yet to refund tuition fee of SC students who got management/NRI medical, engineering seats
content: The Social Welfare Department released ₹20 crore for refunding the fees of these students in the first quarter of the current year’s Budget recently, but it is yet to reach students.

title: Telangana’s outstanding debt rose from ₹90,161 crore to ₹8.21 lakh crore in BRS’s 10-year tenure: Bhatti Vikramarka
content: The Deputy Chief Minister launches trenchant attack on KTR and Harish Rao for their campaign against Telangana Government

title: Ram temple donation theft: RSS calls for Hindu unity, seeks strict action against those guilty
content: RSS general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale cautioned against “conspiracies” by “anti-Hindu, anti-national forces” to exploit the incident and “malign Hinduism and society”

title: ‘When all else fails, people still repose their trust in the judiciary’: INDIA bloc leaders in letter to CJI
content: Signed by leaders of 24 Opposition parties, including independent MP Kapil Sibal, it detailed concerns over the SIR in West Bengal and Bihar; the leaders also made a direct accusation, alleging that they believed recently conducted elections in Delhi, Haryana, and Maharashtra had been “manipulated”

title: Ex-Minister Vaigaichelvan quits AIADMK
content: Mr. Vaigaichelvan had defeated DMK’s senior leader K.K..S.S.R Ramachandran in Aruppukottai in 2011. He lost in 2016 and 2021 Assembly polls.

title: Python rescued from CPI(M) party office in Aryanad in Kerala
content: The office is housed in a sheet-roofed structure, where reptiles are occasionally seen during the monsoon season, forest officials say

title: Operation Numkhor: Customs may summon Dulquer Salmaan again for questioning
content: Among the 60-odd vehicles seized by Customs under the drive are three vehicles owned by Dulquer Salmaan and one which has another owner but which the agency suspects is retained by the actor

title: All the world is a meme: when satire can no longer keep up with reality
content: From The Boys and Game of Thrones to Karuppu and politics in Tamil Nadu, a culture shaped by fandom, memes and star worship are reshaping how we consume stories, heroes and even reality itself, turning critiques into celebrations of the very myths they were meant to dismantle

title: Report vacancies to Kerala Public Service Commission within three weeks, departments and public-sector institutions told
content: Personnel and Administrative Reforms department also asks all departments and public sector institutions to compile and hand over a list of all temporary employees within three weeks

title: GST at 9 — Revenues up 14% in June 2026 but dependence on imports increased, domestic sales lagged
content: As GST marked nine years of implementation on July 1, tax experts point to several aspects — such as input tax credits, dispute resolution, multiple registrations, and the inverted duty structure — as issues that still need to be addressed

title: Study calls for major overhaul of social work education to strengthen disaster preparedness
content: It contends that although social workers have traditionally played an important role in disaster response, their contributions remain largely confined to post-disaster relief and psycho-social support, while their potential in disaster planning, governance and risk reduction remains underutilised

title: After WhatsApp, Telegram and Signal receive notices on username feature
content: The notices follow directions to WhatsApp to pause its username feature over scam and impersonation concerns; Spokespersons for Telegram, Signal did not have an immediate comment to share

title: Karnataka CEO directs BLOs to strictly follow guidelines for door-to-door SIR exercise after complaints
content: A video circulated on social media purportedly showed a Booth Level Agent (BLA) accompanying a BLO during house visits, and distributing political pamphlets

title: GoDaddy fears government’s fake website crackdown could make the internet less safe
content: The legal dispute embroiling GoDaddy and others was triggered ‌by more than 20 companies that sought the court’s intervention on fake websites damaging their brand

title: ‘Gatta Kusthi 2’ movie review: Double the fun, same old pitfalls
content: With sharper writing, Chella Ayyavu’s sequel film improves over the original, only to repeat its biggest mistakes when it matters most

title: Mar Thoma Church head opposes Kerala govt’s move to cut tax on low-alcohol beverages
content: Malankara Mar Thoma Syrian Church head Theodosius Mar Thoma Metropolitan says the Church is against all forms of liquor

title: Plea in Supreme Court seeks an end to ‘coaching Raj’, dummy school nexus
content: Plea urges court to direct authorities to “dismantle the parallel, unregulated, fee-driven private coaching ecosystem” and align the curriculum and testing patterns of national entrance examinations with state-prescribed school syllabus

title: Chessworld AI becomes official technology partner of Karnataka State Chess Association, start-up supported by Elevate
content: Chessworld AI will introduce an integrated digital platform for tournament registrations, player and tournament management, digitisation of over-the-board games, AI-powered game analysis, and personalised learning tools for players.

title: Kochi Corporation’s health standing committee to consider two proposals to use drones for mosquito control
content: A start-up has submitted a proposal for employing drones for spraying larvicides. Another company proposes operating drones to spray an organic compound at mosquito-breeding grounds identified through GIS mapping

title: 'Attacks Being Planned': Gujarat Anti-Terror Agency Arrests 8 Jaish Members
content: The coordinated counter-terrorism crackdown spanned across multiple regions in Gujarat, including Banaskantha, Patan, and Navsari, as well as Dewas in Madhya Pradesh.

title: Centre Summons Meta Officials Over Child Abuse Ads On Instagram: Sources
content: The ministry will seek an explanation from the company -- which owns WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook -- regarding the advertisements.

title: Mohan Yadav's Departments Top Pendency List Ahead Of Assembly Session
content: A total of 1,336 Vidhan Sabha-related matters remain pending across 54 departments and the biggest embarrassment is that two of the five worst-performing departments are directly under Chief Minister Mohan Yadav

title: Congress vs Congress In Punjab: Charanjit Channi Camp Takes On Raja Warring
content: The gathering marks the clearest sign yet of a "Congress vs Congress" battle in Punjab, with the Channi camp pushing for a change in the party's state leadership ahead of the next Assembly elections.

title: BJP Youth Leader Molests Doctor, Threatens To Shoot Her, Case Filed
content: The accused, Anil Srivastava, a member of the BJP State Yuva Morcha executive committee, and his associate have been booked by Sagar police under multiple provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS).

title: ISRO Bomb Threat Email Traced To Ghaziabad, Sender Found Mentally Challenged
content: Bengaluru Police have solved the hoax bomb threat case involving the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) Headquarters after tracing the threatening email to Ghaziabad in Uttar Pradesh.

title: Heavy Rain Forecast For Mumbai, Thane On Weekend, Red Alert Issued
content: Mumbai has been battered by rains throughout this week following the late onset of the monsoon, throwing normal life out of gear.

title: India Clears Rs 52,000-Crore Defence Shopping Cart For All 3 Services
content: The acceptance of necessity (AoN) include the approval of indigenous as well as advanced defence equipment for the armed forces, focusing primarily on air defence, drone-killing equipment, surveillance and unmanned systems

title: 2 Caregivers Arrested For Abusing Children At Bengaluru Daycare
content: Horrifying videos of the abuse were shared by a complainant this week, showing children as young as two years old sitting inside the drum of a front-loading washing machine.

title: 40 Times More Steel Than Eiffel Tower: PM To Launch Mega Refinery Tomorrow
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit Pachpadra in Rajasthan on July 4 to inaugurate the HPCL Rajasthan Refinery Limited (HRRL) refinery, a project expected to significantly boost India's energy security while creating new economic opportunities in the region.

title: "Anti-National Forces Seeking To Malign Hinduism": RSS On Ram Temple Row
content: The RSS said the unfortunate incident of theft of the money deposited in the donation boxes at the temple "has deeply hurt the sentiments and faith of the entire society and millions of Lord Ram's devotees.

title: On CCTV, Woman Steals Jewellery, Threatens To Remove Clothes When Caught
content: The owner of the house in Jaipur's Muhana neighbourhood took a video of her shouting and threatening him

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Story Of Congress And Its Never-Ending Obsession With 'Committees'
content: The present-day Congress works on a mantra that says loud and clear - when in doubt, set up a committee.Whether they achieve anything is another matter.

title: Sale Of Srivari Laddus In Tirumala Tirupati Breaks Record In June
content: During April 2026, a total of 1,11,96,170 laddus were sold, followed by 1,21,35,528 in May 2026, and 1,26,81,805 in June 2026.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Red Alert Issued For Mumbai Tomorrow, Rain In Delhi Today
content: Strong westerly winds likely to prevail over the southern peninsular India and central India till July 8, the India Meteorological Department said.

title: HCLTech Signs $1.14 Billion Deal With European Firm, Shares Open 4.6% Higher
content: The deal will help HCLTech establish an AI-driven operating model to transform and manage the client's global digital workplace and enterprise networks, the company said.

title: Social Media Influencer Stabbed To Death By Husband Over Property Dispute In UP
content: Circle Officer (Sardhana) Ashutosh Kumar said police received information about the murder on Friday morning and rushed to the spot along with senior officers.

title: Woman Kills 3 Children, Then Dies By Suicide In Punjab's Mansa
content: A relative who visited the house on Thursday evening alerted the neighbours and the police about the incident.

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Funeral News LIVE Updates: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's Funeral Begins In Iran
content: Ali Khamenei's Last Rites News Live: Preparations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's public funeral are taking place in Tehran, following which his remains will be taken to the Iraqi holy cities of Najaf and Karbala.

title: 'Madam Is Sleeping': Ex-Diplomat's Call With Benazir Bhutto During 1991 Hijack
content: Kausikan recalled that when he tried to reach Bhutto, he found out she was in Sindh at the time with her family. He said that it was around 3 am when he managed to get the contact of Bhutto's residence with the help of the then Pakistani high commissioner.

title: Wife's 'thousand husbands' barb 'grave provocation' that led to her killing: HC cuts man's life term
content: "When a wife refers to her husband that 'she can keep thousand husbands like him' it is indirect/oblique reference to worthlessness of the husband, meaning he has no value as a human being or a husband; therefore, it can be turned as a sudden and grave provocation"

title: DMK moves Supreme Court to bar TVK leaders from making ‘threatening’ public statements on Karur stampede
content: The DMK petition has pointed out that Chief Minister Vijay’s proposed visit to Karur has the potential to give rise to an apprehension, regarding the fairness and independence of the investigative process.

title: Arvind Kejriwal urges PM to rollback of E20 fuel programme
content: Referring to concerns over fuel efficiency, he claimed that many people were reporting a decline in mileage after switching to E20 petrol

title: Watch: HPV vaccination roll out, climate change, metabolic diseases and more | Health Wrap
content: In this episode of Health Wrap, senior health journalists Ramya Kannan and Zubeda Hamid discuss the week’s important developments in public health. This episode looks at the rollout of Human papillomavirus (HPV) vaccination across States in India to prevent cervical cancer, which continues to claim thousands of lives each year.

title: Can the ED attach a company’s assets after it enters insolvency? | Explained
content: The legal question before the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal was whether the protection available under the IBC moratorium extends to assets that are simultaneously the subject of proceedings under the PMLA

title: Digital arrest scam: CBI arrests three accused after searches in Odisha and Rajasthan
content: The accused have been identified as Bibekananda Dixit and Jayant Kumar Acharya from Balasore, Odisha; and Kanhaiya Lal from Nagaur, Rajasthan

title: Watch: Falling fertility rate, antibiotic resistance and a rare brain infection in Kerala
content: We unpack India’s falling fertility rate and its far-reaching social and economic consequences, examine the growing threat of antibiotic resistance, and assess the impact of recent GST reforms on healthcare. Our Kerala correspondent reports on the challenges of covering cases of amoebic meningoencephalitis, and psychiatrist Dr. Lakshmi Vijayakumar highlights the urgent need for open conversations on youth and mental health.

title: ₹35-crore TVK MLA bribery case: Senthilbalaji’s brother Ashok Kumar moves Madras High Court for anticipatory bail
content: Claims that he has no connection whatsoever with the case and that he had been roped into the matter with a political motive

title: Watch: From Uranium in Breast Milk to Superbugs: AMR Week Explained | Health Wrap
content: In this episode, we will discuss India’s new national plan to combat antimicrobial resistance. On India’s new national plan to combat antimicrobial resistance, uranium being found in the breastmilk of mothers in Bihar, the efficacy of the anti-rabies vaccine, and more.

title: Govt lens on e-rickshaw remote shutdown issue; two apps removed from app stores
